1 import { revalidateTag, unstable_cache } from "next/cache"; 2 import { getAllProducts, syncCatalog, type Product } from "@/lib/db"; 3 4 // ── Cache layer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 5 // The result of getAllProducts is cached under the "products" tag. 6 // Every view that depends on catalog data uses this same tag so that a 7 // single revalidation freshens them all concurrently. 8 const getCachedProducts = unstable_cache( 9 async () => getAllProducts(), 10 ["admin-products-list"], 11 { tags: ["products"] }, 12 ); 13 14 // ── Inline Server Action ───────────────────────────────────────────── 15 // Co-located with the page so the sync trigger and its cache-invalidation 16 // logic are visible right next to the rendered output. 17 async function syncCatalogAction() { 18 "use server"; 19 20 console.log("⏳ Sync triggered — background job dispatched"); 21 22 // Fire the long-running sync in the background WITHOUT awaiting it. 23 // The admin gets immediate control back even though the product list 24 // may reflect pre-sync data for a few more seconds. 25 syncCatalog().catch((err: Error) => { 26 console.error("Background catalog sync failed:", err); 27 }); 28 29 // Mark every fetch / cache entry tagged "products" as stale. 30 // The NEXT render of any consumer (this page, an API route, a different 31 // page) will re-execute its data function and see whatever state the 32 // data layer has at that moment — eventually consistent after the 33 // background sync finishes writing. 34 revalidateTag("products"); 35 } 36 37 // ── Helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 38 39 function statusBadge(status: Product["status"]) { 40 const colors: Record = { 41 active: "#16a34a", 42 inactive: "#ca8a04", 43 discontinued: "#dc2626", 44 }; 45 return ( 46 57 {status} 58 59 ); 60 } 61 62 // ── Page ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 63 64 export default async function ProductsPage() { 65 const products: Product[] = await getCachedProducts(); 66 67 const anySynced = products.some((p) => p.lastSynced !== null); 68 69 return ( 70

4	/**

9	export declare function revalidateTag(tag: string, profile: string | CacheLifeConfig): undefined;
10	/**
11	 * This function allows you to update [cached data](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/caching) on-demand for a specific cache tag.
12	 * This can only be called from within a Server Action to enable read-your-own-writes semantics.
13	 *
14	 * Read more: [Next.js Docs: `updateTag`](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/functions/updateTag)
15	 */
16	export declare function updateTag(tag: string): undefined;
17	/**
18	 * This function allows you to refresh client cache from server actions.
19	 * It's useful as dynamic data can be cached on the client which won't
20	 * be refreshed by updateTag
21	 */
22	export declare function refresh(): void;
23	/**
24	 * This function allows you to purge [cached data](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/caching) on-demand for a specific path.

27	 */
28	export declare function revalidatePath(originalPath: string, type?: 'layout' | 'page'): undefined;
29	export {};

1 import { revalidateTag, unstable_cache } from "next/cache"; 2 import { getAllProducts, syncCatalog, type Product } from "@/lib/db"; 3 4 // ── Cache layer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 5 // The result of getAllProducts is cached under the "products" tag. 6 // Every view that depends on catalog data uses this same tag so that a 7 // single revalidation freshens them all concurrently. 8 const getCachedProducts = unstable_cache( 9 async () => getAllProducts(), 10 ["admin-products-list"], 11 { tags: ["products"] }, 12 ); 13 14 // ── Inline Server Action ───────────────────────────────────────────── 15 // Co-located with the page so the sync trigger and its cache-invalidation 16 // logic are visible right next to the rendered output. 17 async function syncCatalogAction() { 18 "use server"; 19 20 console.log("⏳ Sync triggered — background job dispatched"); 21 22 // Fire the long-running sync in the background WITHOUT awaiting it. 23 // The admin gets immediate control back even though the product list 24 // may reflect pre-sync data for a few more seconds. 25 syncCatalog().catch((err: unknown) => { 26 console.error("Background catalog sync failed:", err); 27 }); 28 29 // Mark every fetch / cache entry tagged "products" as stale. 30 // The NEXT render of any consumer (this page, an API route, a different 31 // page) will re-execute its data function and see whatever state the 32 // data layer has at that moment — eventually consistent after the 33 // background sync finishes writing. 34 revalidateTag("products", { expire: 0 }); 35 } 36 37 // ── Helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 38 39 function statusBadge(status: Product["status"]) { 40 const colors: Record = { 41 active: "#16a34a", 42 inactive: "#ca8a04", 43 discontinued: "#dc2626", 44 };     "content": "# Next.js 16 Server Action no-stale pattern: `updateTag` + `refresh` + `redirect`\n\nNext.js 16.2.0-canary.41 adds `updateTag(tag)` and `refresh()` as the preferred cache-invalidation pair for Server Actions.\n\n- `updateTag('tag')` — server-side cache invalidation with read-your-own-writes semantics. The next server render encountering data tagged with that tag waits synchronously for fresh data (no stale-while-revalidate background trickle). Only callable from Server Actions.\n- `refresh()` — clears the client-side router cache so a subsequent `redirect()` doesn't serve client-cached stale HTML.\n- Pattern: `updateTag('tag')` → `refresh()` → `redirect('/')`.\n\nThis is preferred over `revalidateTag(tag, profile)` for Server Actions because: (a) `revalidateTag` now requires a second",

The tag is the shared invalidation key — any view (catalog, dashboard, stock widget) that tags its cache with `\"products\"` gets invalidated together.\n2. **Sync trigger:** An inline Server Action (`\"use server\"` inside the page component) that calls `revalidateTag(\"products\", {})` then `redirect(\"?synced=true\")`.
